response:
Sec. 1  
Allergies to peanuts are immune system responses to proteins found in peanuts  
These proteins trigger reactions when ingested or contacted  
Living underwater does not eliminate exposure to these proteins  
Peanut proteins can be present in water if contaminated with peanut products  
Allergic reactions are not influenced by the environment in which a person lives  
The body's immune system reacts to allergens regardless of location  

Sec. 2  
Underwater living does not prevent the immune system from recognizing peanut allergens  
Allergic reactions can occur through ingestion inhalation or skin contact  
Water does not act as a barrier against allergens in the same way it does against some pathogens  
People with peanut allergies must avoid allergens regardless of their environment  
Living underwater does not provide protection from peanut allergies  
Therefore people with peanut allergies cannot live underwater due to the risk of exposure to allergens
